task: Fix Discord webhook initialization flow so `askr init` prompts for webhook URL when global config doesn't exist, instead of silently failing.
discussion_summary: The friend ran `askr init` but got a Discord send failure because the webhook wasn't in `~/.config/askr/.env`. Root cause: `setup_keys()` bails early if the global config file exists (even empty), and `askr init` doesn't read the repo's `.env` file. The fix was to make `cmd_init` detect a missing webhook and prompt the user to paste it, saving to the global config. User then questioned whether `askr init` should read from the repo's `.env` first — clarified that the env.py fix now loads global config first, then repo config, so the webhook can come from either source.

accomplishments.what: Modified askr.py cmd_init to detect missing Discord webhook and prompt user to add it during init
Committed changes to env.py and askr.py with fixes for webhook detection and loading order
Pushed changes so friend can pull and re-run askr init with proper webhook prompt

next_actions.action: Friend pulls latest changes and runs `askr init` in project directory — should now be prompted for Discord webhook URL if not in global ~/.config/askr/.env
next_actions.why: Validates the fix works end-to-end; webhook should be saved and next command should post brief to Discord

next_actions.action: Verify Discord brief posts successfully after webhook is saved during init
next_actions.why: Confirms the initialization flow is complete and webhook is being picked up correctly

next_actions.action: Test that subsequent `askr` commands use the webhook without re-prompting
next_actions.why: Ensures env.load() on import correctly reads the saved webhook from global config

next_actions.action: Document the init flow in README: repo `.env` is read as fallback, but global `~/.config/askr/.env` takes precedence
next_actions.why: Clarifies for users where webhook can be stored and which takes priority

decisions.decision: Global `~/.config/askr/.env` is loaded first, then repo `.env` as fallback — not the other way around
decisions.reason: Allows user to override repo settings with personal config; prevents accidental commits of personal webhooks to git

decisions.decision: `askr init` must prompt for webhook if not found in either location, rather than silently failing
decisions.reason: Improves UX — user gets immediate feedback and can fix the issue in one step instead of debugging config files

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Only read webhook from repo `.env` during `askr init`, not from global config
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: but he won't get the project brief for the repo to his discord right? ... shouldn't it take from the .env setup that exists in the repo already?

failed_approaches.approach: Relying on `setup_keys()` to handle webhook prompting during init — it bails early if global config exists
failed_approaches.reason: User never gets prompted if ~/.config/askr/.env already exists, even if webhook is missing or empty

failed_approaches.approach: Only reading repo `.env` during init without checking global config first
failed_approaches.reason: Doesn't allow personal webhook overrides and forces webhook to be committed to git

relational_files.why: Global config file where webhook is saved after user prompts during init
Repo-level config that serves as fallback if global config doesn't have webhook
Uncommitted changes show Phase 4 restructuring — not directly related to this session but in working directory
